description:
Closed registry of disclosure rules. This is where honesty is MECHANIZED.

Every rule fires from a machine-checkable fact in the artifact — never from caller text, and never from a caller flag. That is the whole design: a caller cannot suppress a disclosure by omitting a field, cannot weaken one by rewording it, and cannot promote its data by setting a boolean. The only way to remove a disclosure is to remove the fact that causes it.

The rendered text is generated from `text` here. It appears identically in four places — the artifact JSON, the visible SVG footer, the SVG accessible description, and the table metadata — and a test asserts all four agree.

policy.orderingBySeverity: critical, then important, then informational; within a severity, by rule id. A compact footer shows the highest-priority disclosures and an overflow count; the sidecar and accessible description always contain ALL of them.
policy.neverSuppressible: In stable mode no disclosure can be suppressed, reordered below a caller note, or visually dominated by one.
policy.callerNoteIsNotADisclosure: A caller's declared note is rendered separately, bidi-isolated, under an attribution that marks it unverified. It is never merged into the mandatory disclosure text.

rules.text: Simulation output. These values were produced by a model, not measured from a biological system.
Synthetic fixture. This data was fabricated to exercise the software and carries no scientific meaning whatsoever.
Source unknown. The origin of this data was not declared, so nothing about its provenance can be relied upon.
Extracted from published material. Values were transcribed from a publication and have not been re-derived from primary data.
Manually entered. These values were typed in by hand rather than exported from an instrument or simulator.
Source authenticity not verified. Cortexel validated the structure and semantics of this request; it did not check that the underlying source bytes are what they claim to be.
No independent reference comparison was run for this figure.
Partial network view. This is not the complete network: absent connections here have not been shown to be absent globally.

Rank-local snapshot (rank {rank} of {worldSize}). Under MPI, a rank holds the connections whose target it owns. In-degree is therefore complete for these local targets; out-degree and global completeness are not.
Edges sampled: {retainedConnectionCount} of {sourceConnectionCount} connections are shown. Degree and completeness cannot be read from this figure.
Node set incomplete. Nodes with no drawn edge may still have connections; a node missing here is not a node with degree zero.

rules.trigger: derivation contains a multapse aggregation with countBefore > cells
rules.text: Multiple connections between the same endpoint pair were combined using {aggregation}. The drawn value is an aggregate, not a single synapse.
An empty cell means no connection was observed. It is distinct from a connection whose weight is zero, which is drawn as a value.

rules.trigger: graph layout is not from measured positions
rules.text: Layout is schematic. Node placement was chosen for legibility and carries no spatial meaning.

rules.trigger: spatial figures where some selected nodes have no position
rules.text: {missingCount} of {totalCount} nodes have no declared position and are omitted from the map rather than placed at the origin.
Compacted for display: {countAfter} of {countBefore} observations are drawn, using {policyId}. The complete data is in the attached table and sidecar.
This display policy does not guarantee that a brief transient survives compaction. Read exact values from the table, not from the drawn shape.
The inline table shows {tableRowsInline} of {tableRowsTotal} rows. The complete data is in the sidecar identified by digest.
{excludedCount} events fell outside the declared observation window and are excluded from this analysis.
{missingCount} observations are missing. Lines are broken at each gap; missing values are never interpolated across or drawn as zero.

rules.trigger: derivation contains a unit conversion operation
rules.text: Units were converted during canonicalization: {conversions}. The original values are preserved in the artifact.
No uncertainty is shown ({reason}). The absence of a band means uncertainty was not supplied — not that it is small.

rules.trigger: response curves supplied as aggregates rather than raw repeats
rules.text: Points are aggregates ({estimator}, n = {sampleCount}). The individual repeats were not supplied and cannot be shown.
Kernel-smoothed rate ({kernel}, bandwidth {bandwidth}). This is an estimate, not a literal count per bin, and it is drawn as a continuous line rather than as steps.

rules.trigger: autocorrelation: each event's pairing with itself was excluded
rules.text: Autocorrelation: each event paired with itself is excluded. Distinct events with identical times are retained, so the zero-lag bin is not necessarily empty.
Positive lag means the target train follows the reference train by that lag.

rules.trigger: the caller supplied pre-binned rows rather than raw events
rules.text: Input was pre-binned by the caller. Cortexel re-derived and checked the normalization but did not observe the raw events.
Samples sharing a timestamp were combined using {method}.
The figure carries a note declared by the caller. Cortexel has not verified it.
Experimental; not a canonical Cortexel figure. Its output is not deterministic, not covered by the stable contract, and not accessibility-conformant.

rules.trigger: the active budget profile is not a published default
rules.text: Rendered under a non-default budget profile ({profileId}). This figure does not claim default conformance.
